feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Parameters of Multi action not taking updated story filters
entry.content:
2 story filter on

1-Cost object property (L2)

2-Cost object filter with hierarchy. 

Cascading effect is ON.

I have selected one L2 and accordingly cost object filter getting changed.

I have a multi action which is using Cost object(story filter) as parameter. The issue is it is taking the topmost level of the hierarchy and displaying all L2s under it even though in story filter only 1 L2 is visible.

Is this is a bug where story filters as parameters are not taking updated results.

entry.title: Private Insight rights in SAC
entry.content: Tenant Version 2026.8 According to the SAP Analytics Cloud documentation, users should be able to save Data Analyzer Insights using the "Private Insight" privilege without requiring "Create" on Private Files. In our tenant, the privilege "Private Insight" is not available in the role editor. As a result, granting "Create" on Private Files also allows users to create Stories, which is not the desired behavior. Please clarify whether the privilege is missing due to tenant configuration or product limitation.

entry.title: SAC date variable input as MM/DD/YYYY?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Business users want to enter values for a date variable in MM/DD/YYYY format in SAC story (built on Datasphere models) and I’m looking for guidance regarding date-variable input formatting.

Context:

  • Data is loaded from S/4HANA into the Datasphere Raw layer. Date fields initially arrive as strings in YYYYMMDD format (e.g., 20251231). We manually change the data type of date fields to the "Date".
  • In the Harmonization layer, we consume the Raw layer table without transformations.
  • In the Business layer, we apply cleansing, business logic, master data alignment, and assign semantics to date fields as Calendar Date.
  • In the Consumption layer, we build an Analytic Model and create variables on these date fields.
  • The SAC story is based on this Analytical Model, and the date variables appear as designed
  • The SAC user profile is configured to display dates in MM/DD/YYYY format.

Issue:

When running the story:
  • The variable's value help (possible values) shows the description in MM/DD/YYYY format and the ID displays in YYYY-MM-DD format. User can search in MM/DD/YYYY then select it and the variable show MM/DD/YYYY format (in form of description but accepts the entry as YYYY-MM-DD)
  • If the user manually enters a date as MM/DD/YYYY (without selecting from the value help), SAC throws an error: “Member ID does not exist”.
 

I understand that variables accept the key value rather than the description, but I want to know:

 

Is there any standard SAP-supported way for SAC variables to accept date input in MM/DD/YYYY format, without creating custom fields, casts, or transformations? Since we have many date fields, implementing custom logic repeatedly would be costly, so we’re hoping for a standard approach if one exists.

Thanks in advance for your insights!

Blogs referred:
Assistance Required – Date Format Handling in Data... - SAP Community

Solved: Date shown wrong in SAP Analytics Cloud based on S... - SAP Community

Regards,

Preet

entry.title: Mandatory Nature of the Account Dimension in SAC
entry.content:
1. Assumptions / Objectives

  • We would like to integrate cost data from another model using the key combination of G/L Account × Organization × Business.
  • After the integration, we would like to automatically assign a reporting dimension called “Cost Breakdown Category” by using a Data Action (Advanced Formulas step), based on the above three-key combination.
  • More specifically, our approach is:
    • To maintain the three keys (G/L Account, Organization, Business) as properties (attributes) of each member in the Cost Breakdown Category dimension.
    • To start from records where Cost Breakdown Category is Unassigned (#).
    • To “move” only the records that match the member property conditions to the corresponding Cost Breakdown Category member.
    • After the move, to delete the remaining Unassigned (#) records (i.e., zero them out).

2. Issues / Pain Points

  • Once the above reclassification is completed, from a business perspective we expect that analysis and visualization by G/L account will no longer be necessary.
  • However, due to the structure of the source model, the G/L account exists as the Account dimension.
  • When visualizing a model that includes the Account dimension in a Story, we are experiencing situations where an error occurs / the visualization does not work unless an Account member is selected (e.g., via a filter).
  • We would like to avoid operations where users must select Accounts each time. We want to visualize and analyze data using only the Cost Breakdown Category as the primary dimension.

3. What We Tried / Additional Constraints

  • We considered transferring the data to another model without the Account dimension via Model-to-Model Copy, but the copy step returned an error indicating that a member of the Account dimension must be selected as the context, and therefore the design did not work as intended.
  • In our scenario, we only need costs, and practically do not need non-expense accounts (e.g., revenue).
  • However, because we need to integrate from other models, we would like to use public (shared) dimensions, and we want to avoid recreating an Account dimension with a different hierarchy as much as possible.

4. Questions to Confirm (What We Need to Decide)

We would like to confirm what is possible according to SAC specifications and what the recommended design approach is regarding the following points:

  1. In a model that contains the Account dimension, is there any way to visualize data in a Story without requiring the user to select an Account (i.e., without user interaction)?
    • Examples: fixing a default account at the model level, implicitly fixing it at the story level, or avoiding mandatory selection even if the Account dimension is hidden.
  2. Is our understanding correct that, depending on the model type, the Account dimension becomes a mandatory dimension (i.e., the context must be specified)?
    • If it is mandatory, is it impossible to design it as an optional dimension that can be left unspecified depending on the use case?
  3. Assuming there is a top-level hierarchy such as Expense/Revenue, is it possible to operate the model/story so that it targets expenses only?
    • However, since we want to use public (shared) dimensions, we would like to avoid rebuilding hierarchies or creating separate alternative dimensions as much as possible.
  4. Ultimately, in order to satisfy our requirement (i.e., after reclassification to Cost Breakdown Category, the Account dimension is no longer needed), is there a way (as per SAC specifications) to create a model without the Account dimension (if feasible)?

entry.title: Connection between SAC Analytics Cloud and SAP HANA Cloud to create a model using model import
entry.content: We are trying to create a model in SAC using the model option, but we are attempting to establish a connection with SAP HANA Cloud, which is in BTP, but this has not been possible. We want to clarify the process to follow, whether it is necessary to use the SAP Cloud Connector and the SAC Agent, or if this type of connection is even possible.

entry.title: Location Dimension not visible in SAC Import Model
entry.content:
Step 1

  • Followed SAP Note 3108595 to create Location Dimension from SAC frontend content layer
  • Result: Location Dimension created but "Link Model" option unavailable for Live model

Step 2

  • Created custom table ZBIFNAART001 with SAP standard geo domain types
  • Populated with country-level latitude/longitude values

Step 3

  • Created dimension CDS ZBIFNAARI048 with @analytics.dataCategory: #DIMENSION
  • Added @Semantics.geoLocation.latitude/longitude annotations
  • Associated to I_Country for descriptive text

Step 4

  • Mapped dimension CDS to main cube CDS ZBIFNAARC005
  • Result: Location Dimension still not appearing in SAC model

3. If not supported, what is the recommended architecture for near-real-time geo visualisation in SAC?

entry.title: Deployment of SAP Analytics Cloud to Strengthen the Budgeting Process
entry.content:
Voici la version en anglais :

Deploying SAP Analytics Cloud to Strengthen Our Budgeting Process

As part of our finance transformation journey, we are currently deploying SAP Analytics Cloud to modernize and strengthen our budgeting process.

The main objective of this solution is to enable us to prepare, monitor, consolidate, and analyze the budget in a more structured, collaborative, and efficient way.

Today, the budgeting process requires strong coordination between the different entities, Finance teams, business contributors, and management. SAP Analytics Cloud addresses this need by providing a centralized platform to better organize data, budget assumptions, validations, and analyses.

With this solution, we aim to improve the quality of the budgeting process, reduce manual work, limit the risk of errors, and facilitate the monitoring of each stage of budget preparation.

SAP Analytics Cloud will allow us to work on shared budget models, track versions, analyze variances, produce dashboards, and improve collaboration between all teams involved in the process.

Beyond the tool itself, this project represents an important evolution in the way we prepare the budget. It is about moving toward a more integrated, transparent, and data-driven process.

The success of this deployment will strongly depend on user involvement, the quality of the data entered, compliance with the process steps, and collaboration among all stakeholders.

This project is therefore an important step toward strengthening our budget governance, improving data reliability, and enabling better decision-making.

Through SAP Analytics Cloud, our ambition is to build a budgeting process that is more agile, more reliable, and better aligned with performance management requirements.

entry.title: Query on Creating a Generic OData Connection in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are currently using a custom OData service in our SAP S/4HANA system:

OData Service URL:
https://qs4.sap.abc.com:1234/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_LOCATION_Q01_CDS

This service is being consumed from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using an OData Import Connection.

We would like to know whether SAP Analytics Cloud supports creating a generic OData connection at the system/server level (for example, https://qs4.sap.abc.com:1234) that can be reused to access multiple OData services, rather than creating a separate connection for each individual OData service.

Specifically, we would like clarification on the following:

  1. Is it possible to create a generic OData connection that can consume multiple OData services from the same SAP S/4HANA system?
  2. If yes, what should be the Data Service URL while creating the con

entry.title: Writing (Posting) Data to Non-Leaf Members in Advanced Formulas (Data Action) in SAC Planning
entry.content:
1. Assumptions / Objectives

  • We would like to integrate cost data from another model using the key combination G/L Account × Organization × Business.
  • After the integration, we would like to automatically assign a reporting dimension called “Cost Breakdown Category” by using a Data Action (Advanced Formulas step), based on the above three-key combination.
  • More specifically, our approach is:
    • To maintain the three keys (G/L Account, Organization, Business) as properties (attributes) on each member of the Cost Breakdown Category dimension.
    • To start from records where Cost Breakdown Category is Unassigned (#).
    • To “move” only the records that match the member property conditions to the corresponding Cost Breakdown Category member.
    • After the move, to delete the remaining Unassigned (#) records (i.e., zero them out).

2. Issues / Pain Points

  • Both G/L Account and Organization have hierarchical structures. For the mapping to Cost Breakdown Category, we would like to define the mapping not only by the complete combination of leaf members, but also (when appropriate) at the level of parent (non-leaf) members in the G/L Account hierarchy and the Organization hierarchy.
  • The main purpose is to reduce the number of members in the Cost Breakdown Category dimension and mitigate performance risks.
    • If we build Cost Breakdown Category based on the full leaf-level combination of G/L Account × Organization × Business, the dimension would grow to approximately 800,000 members, which may lead to performance issues.
  • We understand that with CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES = ON, it is possible to read values on non-leaf (calculated) members.
  • However, we do not know whether (and how) we can specify non-leaf members in the G/L Account and Organization hierarchies as the “write target” in Advanced Formulas (e.g., via DATA()), or whether this is supported/recommended at all.

3. Questions (What We Need to Confirm)

  1. In the Advanced Formulas step of a Data Action, is there any way to specify non-leaf members in the G/L Account hierarchy and the Organization hierarchy as the write target?

  2. If writing to non-leaf members is not possible (or not recommended), are there alternative approaches to assign Cost Breakdown Category by using non-leaf members of the G/L Account and Organization hierarchies as mapping criteria?

    • For example: property design on the Cost Breakdown Category dimension, introducing an intermediate dimension, maintaining driver data, splitting the logic into multiple Data Action steps, etc.

  3. If the above alternatives are difficult, do we ultimately need to create a mapping dimension (Cost Breakdown Category) based on leaf-member combinations?

    • If so, could you share any recommended design practices to reduce the performance risk caused by a large number of members (approx. 800,000), such as hierarchy design, attribute-based grouping, or revisiting the mapping granularity?

P.S

For example, we want to assign a flag “Raw Material A” only for the combination of Account = Raw Material Cost, Organization = Procurement Dept., and Business = Gas.

However, “Procurement Dept.” is a non-leaf (parent) member in the organization hierarchy, and it has multiple leaf members underneath it (e.g., Procurement Group, Adjustment Group, and other leaf-level groups).

Therefore, if we model the “classification flag” as a dimension whose member ID represents each leaf-level combination across Account × Organization × Business, we must expand the non-leaf organization into all its leaf members. As a result, the number of combinations explodes, and it would generate approximately 800,000 records (i.e., all possible combinations across the relevant leaf members), which is not scalable from both maintenance and performance perspectives.

entry.title: SAC Dimension API Issue
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are using SAC API to download Cost Center/GL Account/Customer dimension. These APIs exported files in CSV format with comma separator.

We are using comma in the Cost Center/GL Account/Customer description as well which is creating issue here.

Can we change with comma separator in CSV file like | to avoid above issue.

Regards,

Mahesh

entry.title: Difference between SAC Enterprise and SAC Embedded (Known limitations, restrictions)
entry.content:
1. Use case 1: The user wants to know if there're limitations and restrictions when using SAC Embedded Analytics Stories. 
  2. Use case 2: The user wants to know if the feature "Share > Publish to catalog" in SAC Enterprise, enables the consumption of the the SAC Enterprise Story inside S/4HANA. And does it consume a license in SAC Enterprise or no additional fee (as it is embedded in S/4HANA as a native feature)

entry.title: SAC Story Buttons Not Working in Presentation Mode When Accessed via Direct URL in Browser
entry.content:
Users are experiencing an issue with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) stories containing buttons and scripting functionalities. The story operates correctly when opened normally in both View Mode and Presentation Mode. However, when the story's direct document URL is copied and pasted into a web browser, the dashboard becomes unresponsive in Presentation Mode, and buttons embedded in the story no longer function.

Additionally, visual inconsistencies are observed in the dashboard layout, including changes to the background and page formatting.

entry.title: Problem creating connection between SAC & SAP
entry.content:
Kindly be informed that when we try to create a connection between our SAP and new Test SAC Tenant we face the below error while we are doing the same as what we did with PRD SAC Tenant.

 

and please find the attached screenshot as reference.

 

Please support ASAP.

 

Regards,

Ahmed Essam

entry.title: Enable Sorting of Outer Dimension by Measure in Planning Tables Without Blocking Input Readiness
entry.content:
Hi team,

In SAC tables with two nested dimensions on the same axis (e.g., Customer as outer dimension, Version as inner dimension with a fixed logical order), it is currently not possible to sort the outer dimension by a measure value while (a) preserving the inner dimension's fixed block order, and (b) keeping the table input-ready for planning.

For example, Customer 2 should be on the top as it has the highest quantities:

  • "Value Sorting" only works on the innermost dimension(?); it cannot be initiated on the outer dimension.
  • "Rank" can achieve the visual result (sorting Customer blocks while preserving Version order), but it applies a filter/limit mechanism to the whole axis, which disables planning input for the entire table — even when N is set high enough that no rows are actually excluded.

Did I miss something? Could anyone help?

Thanks and Best regards,
Simon

entry.title: SAC Email Server Configuration Error
entry.content:
We are trying to connect an email server in SAC Administration > Notification. We are using the OAuth authentication type, but the connection to the email server fails. The error message we receive is:

“Could not connect to the SMTP server. Please check if the information provided is accurate.”

We followed the documentation step by step Steps to configure Custom SMTP Server on SAP Analy... - SAP Community without any success.

 Do we have a tool that can help identify where the configuration process is getting stuck?

entry.title: SAP SAC Planning Date Dimensions change request
entry.content: Is it possible to have calendar views in Time dimension in SAC for reporting views. Our implementation partner has suggested to use the default dimension which benefits from having better performance and built-in planning features but has major drawback in terms of viewing. For example if the Fiscal year is April- March, then for April 2026 (calendar year) it will show April 2027.We need to know what are the best alternatives in this situation.

entry.title: SAC SMTP (smtp.office365.com) configuration with OAuth
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement to configure SAC - SMTP (smtp.office365.com) with OAuth

So far, I have:

  • Created an application in Azure AD.
  • Assigned the required API permissions.
  • Successfully generated the refresh token in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

However, the SMTP connection is still failing

Regards,

Ganesha

entry.title: Numeric Point Widget does not display % sign for variance although "Display Difference As &#61
entry.content:
We identified an issue with an existing Numeric Point widget.

The widget is configured with:

  • Comparison enabled
  • Display Difference As = Percentage
  • Absolute Base Value enabled

 

Reproducibility

  1. Open existing story.
  2. Open existing Numeric Point widget.
  3. Configure comparison and set "Display Difference As = Percentage".
  4. Save and refresh the story.
  5. Variance displays without % sign.

 

Additional Observation

If a completely new Numeric Point widget is created using the same measure and comparison settings, the percentage sign is displayed correctly.

Therefore, the issue appears to be related to the rendering or metadata persistence of existing widgets rather than the underlying calculation.

 

Expected Result

The Numeric Point widget should displ

entry.title: can we able to reset the story level filters in SAC
entry.content:
can we able to reset story level filters in SAC through script? if yes could you please let me know what function I have to use .

note: not page level filters.

entry.title: Issue with PDF export in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Stories
entry.content:
When I export my SAC Story to PDF, cells containing long text (paragraph-level content) get truncated. Only a portion of the text visible on screen is included in the PDF — the remaining text is lost, even though the underlying data is complete.

I have a Case Activity Log table with columns including:

  • Activity Date
  • Category
  • Type
  • Description
  • Notes ← This column contains multi-paragraph text (medical case notes, RTW forms, patient history, etc.)

What I've Tried

1. Table Widget Styling

  •  Enabled Wrap Text in table cells
  •  Set Row Height to Auto
  • Increased Notes column width to maximum (500+ px)
  •  Reduced font size to 10
  • Result: Table still truncates text visually; PDF still cuts off content

Workaround Attempted: Excel → Word → PDF

  • Exported to XLSX via SAC
  • Opened in Excel → Applied Wrap Text + borders manually
  • Copy-pasted to MS Word (Landscape + A4 + Narrow margins)
  • Saved as PDF from Word
  • Result:  This gives complete content, but it's a manual multi-step process (not user-friendly for business users)

User need to print the report with one click export to pdf without losing data content and human readable font on letter or legal pages.

Just to get more information i made the story as canvas and it still continues to have the issue and cannot print the whole context 

 

Any of the following would be greatly appreciated:

  •  Official SAP documentation on this behavior
  • Native SAC solution or workaround
  •  Best practice for handling large text fields in SAC reports
  •  Confirmation if this is a known limitation
  • Roadmap information for potential improvements

Thank you in advance for any insights!
